_meta.author: Claude autopilot (scorecard-refine-national)
_meta.note: Federal House batch: Mike Thompson (CA-4, refined under new disambiguated slug mike-thompson-ca-04) + CT delegation Courtney (CT-2), DeLauro (CT-3), Himes (CT-4), Hayes (CT-5). All five Democratic incumbents seeking reelection 2026 (Ballotpedia-verified). Evidence from House Clerk roll-call XMLs, members' own press releases, Heritage/SBA/HRC/Giffords/NumbersUSA scorecards, OpenSecrets. Key cross-party deviations: Courtney YEA Laken Riley + HR30 + Police Our Border; Hayes YEA Laken Riley twice then publicly recanted; Thompson + Himes Aye on FIT21 crypto bill; DeLauro genuine Big-Ag-antitrust alignment (JBS).

profile.religion: Catholic (Vote Smart bio; signed 2004 Catholic Democrats' letter opposing communion denial to pro-choice members)
profile.background: US Representative, CA-4 (in the House since 1999; previously CA Senate 1990-98). Vietnam combat veteran (173rd Airborne, Purple Heart), small vineyard owner, Ways & Means member, Blue Dog Coalition, chairs the House Gun Violence Prevention Task Force. Advanced from the June 2, 2026 top-two primary (led with 37%).

mike-thompson-ca-04.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier] Uniformly progressive HARD roll-call record with one partial deviation: Aye on FIT21 crypto market-structure bill (one of 71 Democrats) while still voting against the CBDC ban. Gun-owner/hunter identity (Sportsmen's Caucus co-chair) does NOT translate to a pro-2A record — he chairs the Gun Violence Prevention Task Force, authored H.R.8 universal background checks, voted Yea on the 2022 assault-weapons ban, and is Giffords-endorsed. Did not join Democrat crossovers on Laken Riley, SAVE Act, or HR30. AIPAC funding claims conflict (advocacy tally vs staff denial) — left null. Refined under disambiguated slug mike-thompson-ca-04 (3-way slug collision broken 2026-06-09).

1.note: Yea on Women's Health Protection Act (H.R.3755, 2021, codifying abortion access); Nay on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act twice (H.R.26 2023, H.R.21 2025).

2.note: Yea on Stem Cell Research Enhancement Act (H.R.810, 2005 — federal funding for embryonic stem-cell research).

0.note: Yea on Respect for Marriage Act (H.R.8404, both passage and final concurrence, 2022) — codifying same-sex marriage.

1.note: Yea on Respect for Marriage Act final passage (12/2022).

2.note: Yea on Equality Act (H.R.5, 2021 — adds SOGI to federal civil-rights law).

4.note: HRC Congressional Scorecard 85% (110th Congress); consistent pro-LGBTQ voting record (Equality Act, RFMA).

1.note: No on Parents Bill of Rights Act (H.R.5, 2023 — parental notification/transparency requirements for schools).

0.note: No on CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act (H.R.5403, 2024 — voted against banning a Fed CBDC). Partial deviation: Aye on FIT21 crypto market-structure bill (H.R.4763, one of 71 Democrats).

1.note: Nay on Federal Reserve Transparency Act (Audit the Fed, H.R.24, 2014 — passed 333-92).
No on SAVE Act (proof-of-citizenship voter registration) twice: H.R.8281 (2024) and H.R.22 (2025).

3.note: Yea on H.R.1 For the People Act (2021 — national mail-in voting and registration expansion).

0.note: Nay on H.R.2 Secure the Border Act (2023 — border wall + enforcement package).

1.note: Nay on Laken Riley Act twice (H.R.29 and final S.5, 2025 — not among the 48/46 Democrat crossovers); Nay on H.R.30 Preventing Violence Against Women by Illegal Aliens Act (2025).

3.note: H.R.2 included mandatory E-Verify; he voted Nay.

1.note: Yea on Assault Weapons Ban (H.R.1808, 2022); chairs House Gun Violence Prevention Task Force since 2012; praises red-flag-law funding in the Bipartisan Safer Communities Act.

2.note: Author/lead sponsor of H.R.8 Bipartisan Background Checks Act — expanding federal gun regulation, the documented opposite of repealing NFA/GCA.

4.note: Giffords-endorsed ('his leadership is responsible for so many of the strides...'); signature legislative agenda is expanded federal firearms control.

1.note: Yea on H.R.256 repealing the 2002 Iraq AUMF (2021).

0.note: No on Freedom for Health Care Workers Act (H.R.497, 2023 — ending the CMS COVID vaccine mandate); Nay on Pandemic is Over Act (H.R.382, 2023).

profile.religion: Catholic (Pew Faith on the Hill, 119th Congress)
profile.background: US Representative, CT-2 (since 2007); former state legislator and attorney. Senior Democrat on the House Armed Services Seapower subcommittee, nicknamed 'Two-Sub Joe' for Electric Boat submarine advocacy. Nominated for an 11th term; 2026 primary Aug 11.

joe-courtney.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier] Progressive HARD record on life/marriage/guns/elections (Heritage lifetime 6%) with REAL cross-party deviations on immigration enforcement: YEA on the Laken Riley Act (one of 48 Democrats, Trump's first signed bill of 2025), YEA on H.R.30 (violence by illegal aliens), YEA on the Police Our Border Act (2024) — though he voted Nay on H.R.2 (wall + E-Verify) and later balked at broader enforcement bills, so the deviation is enforcement-targeted, not restrictionist. Genuine credit on anti-debt advocacy: repeatedly sponsored bills to eliminate/zero federal student-loan interest. NRA grade 'D' in 2014 (mildest among CT Dems; dated). Industry-capture caution flagged but not scored: General Dynamics is his top career donor (~$72K PAC + $165K employees + $47K leadership PAC per OpenSecrets-derived reporting) while he leads Seapower and champions Electric Boat contracts.

1.note: Nay on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (H.R.21, 1/2025); voted for the Women's Health Protection Act (own press release); SBA Pro-Life: 'consistently voted to eliminate or prevent protections for the unborn.'

0.note: Yea on Respect for Marriage Act (H.R.8404, both 2022 votes); his own release celebrates codifying same-sex marriage.

1.note: Yea on Respect for Marriage Act final passage (12/2022).

2.note: Yea on Equality Act (H.R.5, 2021); Nay on Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act twice (H.R.734 2023, H.R.28 2025).

1.note: No on Parents Bill of Rights Act (H.R.5, 2023).

0.note: No on CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act (against banning a Fed CBDC) AND No on FIT21 crypto framework (stayed left of 71 Dem crossovers) — contrary on both halves of the cell.

3.note: Repeatedly sponsored bills to eliminate federal student-loan interest (Student Loan Interest Elimination Act; Bank on Students Act with Warren); op-ed 'End the Student Loan Interest Racket' — documented anti-debt-burden advocacy.

1.note: Nay on SAVE Act (H.R.22, 2025 — proof-of-citizenship voter registration); warned it would 'purge voters.'

3.note: Yea on H.R.1 For the People Act (2021 — expands mail voting).

0.note: Nay on H.R.2 Secure the Border Act (2023 — wall + enforcement package).

1.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION, but short of the cell's bar: YEA on Laken Riley Act (one of 48 Dems, mandatory detention of criminal aliens), YEA on H.R.30, YEA on Police Our Border Act — enforcement-aligned crossovers, yet no evidence he supports mandatory deportation of ALL illegal aliens incl. minors. Null, deviation documented.

3.note: H.R.2 included mandatory E-Verify; he voted Nay.

1.note: Yea on Assault Weapons Ban (H.R.1808, 2022 — own release touts it); Yea on H.R.8 universal background checks (2021).
Yea on H.R.256 repealing the 2002 Iraq AUMF (2021).

0.note: No on Freedom for Health Care Workers Act (ending CMS COVID vaccine mandate); Nay on Pandemic is Over Act; Nay on H.J.Res.7 ending the COVID emergency (all 2023).

4.note: Caution flag, not a scored cell: General Dynamics is his top career donor while he leads Seapower and champions Electric Boat contracts — documented conflict-of-interest pattern, but no vote/statement against contractor accountability found.

profile.religion: Catholic (authored 'The Least Among Us,' 2017, framing her politics through faith)
profile.background: US Representative, CT-3 (since 1991); Ranking Member (top Democrat) of House Appropriations. Former executive director of EMILY's List. Running in the Aug 11, 2026 Democratic primary (challenged by Damjan DeNoble and Andrew Rice).

rosa-delauro.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record on life/marriage/guns/elections/border (Heritage 0% in the 119th; NumbersUSA F-). Led the removal of BOTH the Hyde Amendment and the Weldon conscience protections from the FY22 Labor-HHS bill — the conscience-exemption FALSE is on-point and her own initiative. GENUINE cross-party alignment on Big-Ag antitrust: aggressive, documented campaign against meatpacker JBS (demanded USDA debarment over bribery-funded consolidation) and the Expanded Food Safety Investigation Act targeting corporate CAFOs — credit given. Actively OPPOSES raw-milk freedom ('Get it off the shelves — now'). Pro-Israel-lobby funding claims rest on advocacy tallies (TrackAIPAC ~$172K, which drove AIPAC-aligned endorsement of her primary challenger) — left null pending FEC-grade sourcing. Pro-Choice Caucus co-chair title not independently verified.

0.note: Calls the Hyde Amendment 'offensive and discriminatory' and pledged to strip it from all spending bills — documented advocacy for federally funded abortion, contrary to conception-personhood affirmation.

1.note: Nay on Born-Alive Act (2023); Yea on Women's Health Protection Act (2021) and cosponsor of WHPA 2025 (H.R.12); as Appropriations chair removed Hyde from FY22 Labor-HHS — first bill without it since 1977.

2.note: Aye on Stem Cell Research Enhancement Act (H.R.3, 2007 — federal funding for embryonic stem-cell research).

4.note: Former executive director of EMILY's List (abortion-rights PAC) — organizational leadership documented, but no itemized donation evidence pulled this pass; null on the funding question specifically.

0.note: Yea on Respect for Marriage Act (2022); own release: 'DeLauro Votes to Protect Same-Sex Marriage Equality.'

1.note: Yea on Respect for Marriage Act final passage (12/2022).

4.note: Self-described 'proud member of the Congressional Equality Caucus,' dedicated to advancing LGBTQ+ policy — documented contrary to opposing its promotion in public policy.

1.note: No on Parents Bill of Rights Act (H.R.5, 2023).
Her FY22 Labor-HHS bill removed the Weldon Amendment — the conscience protection for medical providers who decline to participate in abortion.

0.note: No on CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act (against banning a Fed CBDC); No on FIT21 crypto framework.

1.note: Nay on Federal Reserve Transparency Act (Audit the Fed, H.R.24, 2014).
No on SAVE Act (H.R.8281, 2024 — proof-of-citizenship voter registration).

3.note: Yea on H.R.1 For the People Act (2021).

0.note: Nay on H.R.2 Secure the Border Act (2023).

1.note: Nay on Laken Riley Act final passage (S.5, 1/2025) — did not join the 46 Democrat crossovers.

3.note: Nay on H.R.2 (included mandatory E-Verify); NumbersUSA grade F- (4%); has not cosponsored the Legal Workforce Act.

1.note: Yea on Assault Weapons Ban (2022); Yea on H.R.8 universal background checks (2021); NRA grade F.
Yea on H.R.256 repealing the 2002 Iraq AUMF (2021).

2.note: Documented, sustained Big-Ag-antitrust campaign: condemned USDA failure to investigate 'corrupt meatpacker JBS,' demanded debarment over bribery-funded consolidation, noted JBS took $100M+ in farmer trade aid; introduced Expanded Food Safety Investigation Act targeting corporate CAFOs. Genuine cross-party alignment — credit earned.

3.note: Actively opposes raw-milk freedom: demanded FDA pull Raw Farm products ('Get it off the shelves — now') and pressed HHS to tell Americans not to drink raw milk.

profile.religion: Presbyterian (elder, First Presbyterian Church of Greenwich, PCUSA)
profile.background: US Representative, CT-4 (since 2009); Ranking Member, House Intelligence Committee. Former Goldman Sachs vice president and affordable-housing nonprofit executive. New Democrat finance moderate; declared for reelection, 2026 primary Aug 11.

jim-himes.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier] Progressive HARD record on life/marriage/guns/elections/border (Heritage 0%, 117th). Distinctive economic profile: AUTHORED a pro-CBDC white paper ('Winning the Future of Money,' 2022) proposing a Fed digital dollar, and voted against the CBDC ban — while crossing party FOR FIT21 crypto market-structure (one of 71 Dems). Leads FISA Section 702 reauthorization (Aye on RISAA 2024; championed 2026 reauth, calling 702 'our country's most important and irreplaceable foreign intelligence collection tool') — surveillance-state alignment noted. AIPAC was his top contributor in the 2022 cycle (OpenSecrets-derived). Securities & Investment his top donor industry ($458,906, 2023-24). Did NOT join Dem crossovers on Laken Riley, SAVE Act, Born-Alive, or girls' sports.

0.note: Official 'Women's Health and Rights' page supports abortion rights; Planned Parenthood Action Fund 100% rating — documented contrary to conception-personhood affirmation.

1.note: Yea on Women's Health Protection Act (2021); Nay on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (H.R.21, 2025); SBA: 'consistently voted to eliminate or prevent protections for the unborn.'

0.note: Yea on Respect for Marriage Act (12/2022).

1.note: Yea on Respect for Marriage Act final passage.

2.note: Yea on Equality Act (2021); Nay on Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (H.R.28, 2025).

1.note: No on Parents Bill of Rights Act (H.R.5, 2023).

0.note: Authored the pro-CBDC white paper 'Winning the Future of Money' (2022) proposing a Fed-issued digital dollar; No on the CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act (2024). Partial deviation: Aye on FIT21 crypto bill (one of 71 Dems; called it 'an important step forward' — coindesk.com 5/22/2024).

1.note: Nay on SAVE Act (H.R.22, 2025) — not among the 4 Democrat crossovers.

3.note: Yea on H.R.1 For the People Act (2021).

0.note: Nay on H.R.2 Secure the Border Act (2023 — wall + mandatory E-Verify).

1.note: Nay on Laken Riley Act (H.R.29, 2025 — 48 Dems crossed; Himes did not); Nay on Police Our Border Act (2024).

3.note: H.R.2 included mandatory E-Verify; he voted Nay.

1.note: Yea on H.R.8 universal background checks (2021); Yea on Assault Weapons Ban (H.R.1808, 2022); campaign site boasts 'taking on... the NRA.'
Yea on H.R.256 repealing the 2002 Iraq AUMF (2021).

3.note: AIPAC was his top contributor in the 2022 cycle (OpenSecrets-derived; FEC-documented PAC giving).

0.note: Nay on Pandemic is Over Act, Nay on ending the COVID national emergency (H.J.Res.7), No on Freedom for Health Care Workers Act (all 2023) — pro-mandate positions.

profile.religion: Methodist (Pew Faith on the Hill, 119th Congress)
profile.background: US Representative, CT-5 (since 2019); Connecticut's first Black congresswoman. 2016 National Teacher of the Year; teen mother from Waterbury public housing. Ranking Member, House Agriculture Subcommittee on Nutrition. Running in the Aug 11, 2026 Democratic primary.

jahana-hayes.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, federal tier] Progressive HARD record (Heritage 5%, 118th) with the batch's biggest deviation story: voted YEA on the Laken Riley Act TWICE (H.R.29 and final S.5, Jan 2025 — one of ~46-48 Democrats), then publicly recanted at an April 2025 town hall ('It's a vote that I regret') — both the votes and the recantation are documented; cell left null as the positions cancel, full audit trail here. Genuine credit on small-farm advocacy: Ag Nutrition Ranking Member who offered five 2026 farm-bill amendments incl. crop-insurance reform for small farmers and said the bill 'continues to abandon small farms in Connecticut.' Her teen-mother/faith story does NOT translate into any pro-life policy position — voting record uniformly pro-abortion-rights (EMILY's List $129,267 in 2023-24). Called private school vouchers 'absolutely a sham' in committee; NEA-recommended 2026. Sponsored a 2021 bill to eliminate religious-refusal conscience exemptions in health services.

0.note: Direct quote: 'I support a woman's right to choose' — documented contrary to conception-personhood affirmation.

1.note: Nay on Born-Alive Act (2023); Yea on Women's Health Protection Act (2021), cosponsored WHPA 2023 and signed its discharge petition.

4.note: EMILY's List $129,267 in the 2023-24 cycle (OpenSecrets) — abortion-industry PAC funding accepted.

0.note: Yea on Respect for Marriage Act final passage (12/2022).

1.note: Yea on Respect for Marriage Act.

2.note: Yea on Equality Act (2021); her release celebrates it as a 'historic civil rights bill.'

4.note: Perfect score on the HRC Congressional Scorecard, 118th Congress.

0.note: Called private school voucher programs 'absolutely a sham' in committee (career public-school teacher); NEA-recommended candidate 2026.

1.note: No on Parents Bill of Rights Act (2023); denounced it as an 'extreme proposal' ('Politics Over Parents').
Introduced a bill to eliminate refusal of health services based on religious beliefs (2021) — sponsored legislation stripping conscience exemptions.

0.note: No on CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act (against banning a Fed CBDC); No on FIT21 crypto framework.

1.note: Nay on SAVE Act (2025); called it 'legislation to block Americans from the ballot box.'

3.note: Yea on H.R.1 For the People Act (2021).

0.note: Nay on H.R.2 Secure the Border Act (2023).

1.note: KEY DEVIATION, then recanted: YEA on Laken Riley Act twice (H.R.29 1/7/2025 and final S.5 1/22/2025, one of ~46-48 Democrats), but publicly reversed at an April 2025 town hall: 'It's a vote that I regret.' Vote and recantation cancel — null with full documentation.

3.note: H.R.2 included mandatory E-Verify; she voted Nay.

1.note: Yea on H.R.8 universal background checks (2021); Yea on Assault Weapons Ban (2022 — own release: 'Hayes Votes to Ban Assault Weapons').

4.note: Giffords endorsee; Deputy Whip of the Gun Violence Prevention Task Force — documented gun-control advocacy.

1.note: Yea on H.R.256 repealing the 2002 Iraq AUMF (2021), with her own supporting statement.

0.note: No on Freedom for Health Care Workers Act (H.R.497, 2023 — ending the CMS COVID vaccine mandate).

3.note: Documented, repeated small-farm advocacy: offered five 2026 farm-bill amendments incl. crop-insurance reform for small farmers; said the farm bill 'continues to abandon small farms in Connecticut.' Credit on the small-farm-protections component; no raw-milk position found.
